purpose: Project the toy temporal-alignment collapse outputs onto a surrogate NIST-like D-line frequency scale and test whether the current internal step resolution could honestly be considered within NIST experimental calibration/uncertainty scale.

inputs.toy_mapping: Use r5 and r6 onset separation (13 steps) as a surrogate image of the neutral lithium D1-D2 separation; this is explicitly a dimensional calibration convenience, not a physical derivation.

honesty_note: This is a surrogate dimensional comparison, not a real spectroscopic prediction. It tells us whether the current toy time-step/lock-step granularity is even remotely fine enough to talk about NIST-level uncertainty. It does not validate the model against lithium spectroscopy.
